reviews.review_description: Add a outside environment for that cafe window.

congratulations to the developer because now the artificial intelligence finally understands Italian well.
This is the first AI app on the Quest2 that finally understands Italian.

later in development I would like to be able to choose between other personalities and perhaps other bartenders

I would also like animations to be implemented, that the bartender could move and walk.

But if resuscitations are not convincing, it is better to devote ourselves to some other aspect of development for the moment.

in fact I am very demanding about animations, and I would like to see beautiful animations, where you don't see the gap between one animation and another, and the girl's movement is all one without any interruptions or strange jerks.

I would like some natural animation perhaps using the motion capture technique

for the bar it would be nice if strangers or friends could come in, not many, maybe three people at most, or you can choose not to let anyone in (in the options). the human avatar would have to be complete with legs.

these are just wishes or requests, but the important thing is that the App works well, now that it finally works well we need to make it more interesting. But the improvements must always concern the artificial intelligence and its avatar and then the various secondary aspects, thanks for this virtual bar, I will continue to follow it with interest

I've been looking for an app like this and it is certainly fun, at least on a rudimentary level. I really enjoyed talking to Rosa about her parents, lol.  She definitely is NOT an AI powered NPC. 

Biggest bug I found is that the girl just cannot serve a pink doughnut. She gives you a latte instead. If you tell her it's wrong, she apologizes, says she'll take it back abs give you another but she does not take it back and just gives you another latte.  Didn't ruin my enjoyment of talking to an AI though. When I told her I couldn't get through the door, she did tell me to follow her to the door...  and she doesn't move. 

I bought some points, so keep making this app better, please.  Good work so far.
Wth is this turd of an app even allowed on here. Thank god it's free.
How do you talk to that lady?????
Very boring, might as well just talk to Alexa, she can’t do much.

1.- Name it "GPT" and charge for general use no matter if theres plenty of AI free projects instead.
2.- Add a ver very annoying element like a sit that relocate the user on fade out effect no matter if users move an inch, just apply it, people love annoying stuff in a 3" room.
3.- Add physics without consider general use with hands, that make try to hold a cup something impossible.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE:
Thanks to your feedback, we've rolled out daily free credits for everyone to dive into our service hassle-free. Plus, we've spruced up our grab physics and are on a mission to improve things further!
-----------------------------------

Hey confusionaut,

1: I'm really sorry, but to keep offering our AI services, we need to cover our operational costs, which is why we can't provide this service for free. While there are some free AI services out there, we've found that relying on OpenAI ensures excellent multi-language support. Their model has been the most reliable in our tests, and we want to make sure all users feel included.

2: I totally get where you're coming from on this. We're on it and we'll make sure to test and improve it for the next update. Hang tight!

3: I'll take a closer look at it and tweak the physics to make things smoother for you.

Thanks for your review! Your feedback is noted, and we're working on improvements for the next update. Stay tuned for a better experience! 

If you have additional feedback or need support, feel free to reach out to me at ft_studio@outlook.de.

Thank you,
Tom

reviews.review_description:
Very buggy and there was no way to adjust height and no settings yet for anything such as comfort.

Tokens are expensive, costing you about 1 cent per reply if you buy the cheapest/smallest 100 token package. Processing of the responses was also really slow. It could take up to half a minute to get a reply to "I'd like a coffee, please". 

There wasn't a lot of personality developed for the AI waiter. It was pretty bare-bones 2015-era AI chatbot level of conversational skills at best.

Also I didn't get anything when I ordered stuff after getting my first cup of coffee. The chatbot instead wasted tokens with a reply that I assume is a name of the function in the code such as "ServeCoffee" or "ServePinkDonut".

Where's my pink donut Rose??

UPDATE: Thank you for a swift response Dev! I checked out the updated version today and the pricing is indeed more generous and I even got my donut! Still takes one credit per response so for me it costs the same as before still though.

Now the pricing is not outrageous, costing about 2 cents per 5 responses. Based on my understanding about GPT API and cloud text-to-speech pricing, it is now way less greedy in relation to token/character prices. 

Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body:
Hey there,

Thanks a bunch for your awesome review! Your feedback is like gold to me. Sorry for any hiccups with the app, but your input is super crucial, especially in this early access phase.

I'm thrilled to share that we've just rolled out a brand-new update packed with exciting improvements. We've significantly lowered prices, introduced a more comfortable seat for seated gameplay, enhanced AI conversations for smoother interactions, slightly improved response times, and sprinkled in some more fun features.

Once again, thank you for being a part of this journey and for sharing your thoughts. Your support and feedback play a pivotal role in shaping the app's evolution, and I'm truly grateful for your engagement.

Cheers,
Tom
